q: Which set correctly lists core Microsoft Power Platform products?
o: Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, Power Pages, Copilot Studio
Azure Functions, Logic Apps, Service Bus
why: Power Platform comprises Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, Power Pages and Copilot Studio over Dataverse.

q: Which Power Platform product is designed to build EXTERNAL-facing websites over Dataverse data?
why: Power Pages builds secure external websites on Dataverse; the others target apps, automation, analytics and copilots.

q: As a developer, when should you write CODE rather than use configuration?
o: Only when configuration can't meet the requirement
Always, code is faster to maintain
why: The guidance is to prefer configuration and reserve code for what configuration can't achieve.

q: Decide whether each statement about Power Platform for developers is true.

rows.s: Power Automate automates processes with cloud flows.
Power BI is primarily for building data visualizations and reports.
Copilot Studio is used to package managed solutions.

why: Automate = flows, Power BI = analytics, Copilot Studio = conversational copilots (not solution packaging).

q: What is the role of a connector in Power Platform?
o: A proxy that lets apps/flows talk to an external service or API
why: Connectors expose external services/APIs to Power Platform through their operations.

q: Which describes the difference between standard and premium connectors?
o: Premium connectors require a premium license/plan; standard ones are broadly included
Standard connectors need code; premium don't
why: Premium connectors (e.g., many enterprise/SQL/custom) require premium licensing; standard connectors are more broadly available.

q: When no built-in connector exists for your API, what can a developer build?
why: Custom connectors wrap arbitrary REST APIs when no prebuilt connector exists.

q: Decide whether each connector statement is true.

rows.s: A single connector definition can back many connections.

why: Connectors expose actions/triggers; each authenticated connection instantiates the connector.

q: Dataverse provides a security model built on which primary construct?
why: Dataverse security uses roles granting privileges at table and record levels, with teams and business units.

q: Which principle should guide privilege assignment in Dataverse?
o: Least privilege — grant only what's needed
why: Apply least privilege: give the minimum access required for the task.

q: Decide whether each security statement is true.

rows.s: Teams can be used to share access among multiple users.
Business units help organize security boundaries.
Security is enforced only in the UI, not the API.

why: Teams/business units structure access; Dataverse enforces security at the platform (API) level, not just UI.

q: The Common Data Model (CDM) primarily provides...
o: Standardized table and column definitions with shared semantics
why: CDM standardizes entities/attributes so data is consistent and interoperable.

q: Why would a developer 'consume' the Common Data Model?
o: To reuse standard schemas instead of reinventing table structures
why: Consuming CDM reuses well-defined standard schemas, saving design effort and aiding interoperability.

q: Which is a benefit of storing business data in Dataverse?
o: Built-in security, relationships, and rich metadata across apps
It removes the need for any UI
why: Dataverse offers security, relationships, business logic and metadata usable across the platform.

q: Decide whether each CDM/Dataverse statement is true.

rows.s: Dataverse can work with many data types and app types.
CDM promotes consistent semantics across solutions.

why: Dataverse is broad and metadata-driven; CDM standardizes meaning across solutions.

q: Which extensibility layer changes the USER EXPERIENCE of a model-driven app?
why: UX extensibility uses client script and PCF; plug-ins/Service Bus are server/integration-side.

q: Which Azure services support large-scale analytics over Dataverse data?
o: Azure Data Lake and Azure Synapse Analytics (via Synapse Link)
why: Azure Synapse Link exports Dataverse data to a Data Lake for analytics in Synapse.

q: Synapse Link for Dataverse is mainly used to...
o: Continuously export Dataverse data for analytics without impacting the transactional store
why: Synapse Link replicates Dataverse data to a lake so analytics run separately from the operational store.

q: Decide whether each analytics statement is true.

rows.s: Azure Synapse Analytics can query data exported from Dataverse.
A Data Lake is a suitable target for large analytical datasets.
You must copy data manually every day for Synapse Link to work.

why: Synapse Link automates continuous export to a Data Lake queryable by Synapse.

q: A developer must choose where to add complex server-side business logic. Which is the platform's data+logic core?
why: Dataverse is the data and server-logic core (business rules, plug-ins, security) of Power Platform.
